cells.1: STRUCTURAL TYPOLOGY · 19-SECTION CLIENT REPORT TEMPLATE · v3.1

Phase 3 File 2 of 5 · Build date: 2026-05-06 · Schema: ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx v1.0 · Methodology: ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx v1.1 · Status: Internal design specification — requires field testing before claiming validation

This file specifies the structure of the engagement-priced ($90K-$200K) M&A integration-risk client report. It defines 19 sections covering: Executive Summary; The Deal; Methodology & Evidence Base; Acquirer and Target environment diagnoses; Cross-Side Comparison; Environment Compatibility Score (ECS); ten Risk Output sections (one per canonical risk category per schema sheet 13); Pre-Close Actions & Post-Close Integration Roadmap; Audit Trail & Sealed Prediction Lock.

This template is NOT the self-serve web product output. The Step 3 self-serve UI specification was extracted to ST_Step3_Output_Screens_Spec.xlsx on May 6, 2026 by user authorisation. This template is also NOT a practitioner diagnostic report (which centres on individual MBTI Type / Persona / Shadow analysis) — this is the M&A integration-risk product. The two products serve different audiences and engagement tiers.

cells.1: Authored last (after Sections 2-19 complete). Length: 1-2 pages maximum. Audience: deal lead, CIO, board. Purpose: enable a senior decision-maker to understand the diagnosis and required actions in five minutes.

This diagnostic does not rank people, functions, or types by human worth. It evaluates the fit between observed decision patterns and a specific M&A integration problem. Environment names are operational classifiers describing how an organisation's decision logic and resource allocation function — they are not moral judgments about the people who work in those organisations.

cells.3: One sentence: the single most important diagnostic conclusion. Format: "[Acquirer signal pattern] acquiring [Target signal pattern] at ECS [score]: [highest-severity risk category] is [severity]."

Three Risk Outputs at highest severity. For each: category label + one-sentence finding from the corresponding Risk Output coverageNote.
If this engagement is a sealed prediction: locked ECS value + delta + verification window + senior partner sign-off date. If not: "This engagement is not a sealed prediction."
Three highest-priority preCloseActions aggregated from Risk Outputs. Each action: actor + verb + outcome.
Three highest-priority postCloseActions aggregated from Risk Outputs.

cells.1: Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1 (Three-Layer Architecture) and §11 (Methodology Position Statement). Audience: any reader needing to assess the report's evidentiary basis. Purpose: make the diagnosis auditable and defensible under expert review.

The Structural Typology M&A integration-risk diagnostic does not treat respondent answers as factual truth. It treats them as structured behavioural evidence: what the respondent can acknowledge, what they rationalise, what they cannot describe, and where their account diverges from other respondents or from documentary evidence. Lying, evasion, rationalisation, and cross-respondent contradiction are not failure modes of the instrument — they are part of the diagnostic material. The instrument is designed to make these patterns visible before they become integration failures.

cells.1: From ST_ECS_v2_canonical.xlsx (March 2026 canonical version). Case study §4 convention. Note: ECS is a specification, not a verdict — Amazon-Whole Foods succeeded at ECS 48.3 while AECOM-URS failed at ECS 58.8. Integration protocol selection matters independently of score.

ECS is calculated only after Layer 3 (Analyst Review Gate, Step 4.7) confirms the acquirer signal direction AND the target signal direction. Correct order: Layer 1 signal pattern + Layer 2 evidence + Layer 3 analyst confirmation -> ECS -> Risk Output. NOT: survey result -> ECS -> conclusion.

Probability the integration produces a structural break in the combined organisation: authority mechanisms incompatible at the governance level; decision rights cannot be exercised across the combined entity without re-litigation; cultural integration fails because the operating logics are mutually exclusive.

Acquirer authority mechanism + Target acquiescence pattern + Cross-side divergence on Axis 1 (Authority) and Axis 5 (Resource Flow). Most strongly indicated when AEM Q1 and TSAM Q1 produce different signal directions.

Per ST_Friction_Point_Lookup_updated.xlsx: this risk category is most commonly tagged as FP1 (Days 30-60) for pair combinations where authority mechanisms differ at the governance layer. Pair tag is the FLOOR: analyst may compose additional Analyst Assessments based on engagement-specific evidence.

clientJourney.description: Client reads the Due Diligence page. Identifies with the problem: management team performance degradation after M&A close. Clicks "Start the Environment Diagnostic" CTA.

Before any survey instrument, the respondent identifies their organisational role in the transaction. ▶ Track 1: Acquirer side (CFO / CHRO / Deal Lead). ▶ Track 2: Target side (CEO / Founder / Senior Operator). Routing only — no instrument file.

Acquirer's senior team completes the v3.1 11-question self-identification instrument (Q11 added v3.1: NF/SFP discriminator). Per-question Direct Observation Gate + evidence-machinery fields (evidenceType, knowledgeLevel, confidence, reliabilityFlags). Output: weighted signal pattern (NOT a final environment label per methodology §1).

clientJourney.step: Environment Diagnostic v2 (Layer 1, target external observation)
clientJourney.description: Two-level behavioural questionnaire covering all 9 interaction environments. Level 1: Screening (12 Q / 6 axes) — reduces field to 2-3 candidate signal directions. Level 2: Deepening (10 Q targeted at the 4 most discriminating pair tests). v3.1: thin-coverage warnings active for NT/STP, NF/NT, NF/SFP. Output: weighted signal pattern.

Completed exclusively by Track 2 respondents (target organisation). Mirror instrument to Step 2-A — same 11 questions, same evidence-machinery, with acquisition_framing_contamination check by design. Per schema sheet 16_OverridePrecedence: target self-assessment never overrides target-observer evidence (Step 2-B + Step 2-D below) without explicit per-deal documentation.

NEW PATH IN v3.1: acquirer-side respondent answers the 23-question Target Observed Diagnostic. Captures what the acquirer observes about the target — facade-vs-core gap detection (EVID Q4) is the primary deliverable. Output: signal pattern + facade-vs-core gap classification (strategic gap / compliance gap / no gap).

All Layer 1 instruments produce simultaneous output. Per ST_Dual_Respondent_Axis_Comparison_v1.xlsx: cross-respondent agreement classified into one of four states (① CONVERGENT / ② PARTIAL CONVERGENCE / ③ ROLE-LEVEL SPLIT / ④-A or ④-B IRRESOLVABLE). Per ST_Triage_Framework.xlsx: triage by reliability-flag count + contradiction count + evidence-quality multiplier. Free-tier output: directional pattern only — preliminary signal at confidence-limit level (per methodology §3 Gate 3 / per ST_Free_Tier_Output_Narratives_updated.xlsx Confidence_Gate_Mapping). NO final environment lock — environment patterns inform the analyst, they are not the deliverable.
clientJourney.threeLayerPosition: Layer 1 reconciliation only — no Layer 3 review at this point

clientJourney.description: Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §3 Gate 1 (Analyst Gate): free-tier directional indicator may be self-serve; engagement-priced reports require analyst review. Client decides: (a) accept directional pattern as sufficient (no further engagement); (b) commission engagement-priced full report ($90K-$200K — proceeds to Step 4); (c) commission sealed prediction (proceeds to Step 4 with sealed-prediction protocol per methodology §9). Triage Framework Trigger 5 / 6 may force this gate to (b) or (c) if reliability or contradiction tier requires it.
clientJourney.threeLayerPosition: Decision gate — between free-tier and paid tiers

clientJourney.description: Administered when Step 3 returns ② PARTIAL CONVERGENCE or co-presence signal AND triage framework Triggers 1/2/3 fire (signal weak or co-present, but evidence quality permits instrument-level resolution). NOT served when Triggers 5 (reliability saturation) or 6 (contradiction blocking) fire — those routes go directly to Layer 3 analyst review or practitioner. Target: 1-2 candidate environments. Administered to 8-15 mid-level managers + long-tenure employees. Output: signal pattern, NOT a final environment lock per ST_Triage_Framework.xlsx sheet 6_Verification_Output.
clientJourney.projectFilesUsed: 9× [ENV]_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx (NF_NT, NF_SFJ, NF_SFP, NT_STJ, NT_STP, SFJ_SFP, SFP_SFJ, STJ_STP, STP_STJ) + 9× Branded_Questionnaire variants (certified practitioner versions)
clientJourney.costTime: Included in engagement · ~2-3 hrs admin per organisation

clientJourney.description: Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §1 (Three-Layer Architecture): documentary evidence is the second-layer input that governs over respondent self-report when the two diverge (per schema sheet 16_OverridePrecedence). Evidence categories: governance materials (board minutes, charters, audit reports), dataroom files, public records, interview transcripts, board observer access logs, compensation data, regulatory filings. Each evidence item recorded per schema sheet 9_EvidenceItem_Record with provenance + reviewStatus (unreviewed / verified / disputed). Layer 2 evidence is the primary signal source for the governance_risk Risk Output category and the override basis for any answer_vs_document Contradiction record.
clientJourney.projectFilesUsed: ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx sheet 9_EvidenceItem_Record (record schema) + sheet 16_OverridePrecedence (override rules) + dataroom access (engagement-specific)
clientJourney.costTime: Included in engagement · ~3-5 days collection + review

clientJourney.description: Per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §3 Gate 3 (Confidence Gate) + §5 (Contradiction Triage) + §4 (Assessment Authoring): analyst reviews approved Layer 1 answer records + reviewed Layer 2 evidence items + Contradiction records identifying tensions between them. Authors Analyst Assessment records per §4 procedure. Resolves or escalates Contradiction records per §5. Verifies Confidence Gate threshold: 5 conditions checked (direct-observation share ≥60%, triangulation, documentary corroboration, reliability flag rate <20%, full Tier role coverage). Confidence cap applied. THIS IS THE GATE. No engagement-priced output proceeds without analyst approval (per §3 Gate 1 row 2).
clientJourney.costTime: Included in engagement · ~5-8 days analyst time

clientJourney.description: Two confirmed signal directions (acquirer + target — confirmed by analyst at Step 4.7) → ECS computation per ST_ECS_v2_canonical.xlsx (March 2026 canonical, supersedes _updated and _full_varians_matrix). Formula: ECS = 100 − (weighted conflict / max conflict × 100). For each of 17 resources: net flow direction, magnitude, deal-type weight (Platform / Roll-Up / Bolt-On). Output: ECS score (0-100) + risk band + integration protocol recommendation.
clientJourney.projectFilesUsed: ST_ECS_v2_canonical.xlsx (ECS Calculator + Compatibility Matrix 9×9 + Resource Impact Matrix + Deal Type Weights)
clientJourney.threeLayerPosition: Layer 3 — composite computation from approved Layer 3 findings

clientJourney.description: Identify 3-5 dominant personality types in the leadership layer of each organisation. For each type × each environment: NATURAL / FUNCTIONAL / STRESS / RISK / NEUROSIS state per Most_important_file__MBTI_Dynamic_Hierarchy_v2.xlsx. Output: per-leader Persona/Shadow analysis + integration-stress prediction. NOTE: this step is type-mapping for individual leaders, distinct from the organisational environment diagnosis at Steps 2-A through 4.7.
clientJourney.threeLayerPosition: Layer 1 + Layer 3 (per-leader analysis under Layer 3 supervision)
clientJourney.costTime: Included in engagement · ~37 min per leader (self-serve)

clientJourney.description: Engagement deliverable. 19-section structure per ST_B_Single_Output_Template_v1.xlsx: Sections 1-7 diagnostic layer (Executive Summary; The Deal; Methodology & Evidence Base; Acquirer Environment Diagnosis; Target Environment Diagnosis; Cross-Side Comparison & Contradictions; ECS); Sections 8-17 Risk Output layer (one per canonical risk category per schema sheet 13: integration_fracture, leadership_accountability, key_person_retention, founder_dependency, decision_rights_conflict, governance_risk, political_protection, talent_flight, management_performance_drop, months_6_18_failure); Section 18 actions roadmap; Section 19 audit trail + sealed prediction lock.
clientJourney.projectFilesUsed: ST_B_Single_Output_Template_v1.xlsx (19-section template) + ST_Friction_Point_Lookup_updated.xlsx (FP1/FP2/FP3 per pair + risk-category tags)
clientJourney.costTime: Included in engagement · Delivered within 2 weeks of Step 4.7 completion

clientJourney.description: Conditional step: only for sealed-prediction engagements (per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §9). Senior partner reviews entire chain (respondent answers, documentary evidence, contradiction resolutions, Analyst Assessments, Risk Output records, ECS computation). Senior partner sign-off statement signed and timestamped. Prediction sealed: ECS value locked, delta locked, supporting Risk Output IDs locked. Recorded in ST_Prediction_Ledger_v1.xlsx. Forward-verification clock starts. Reference: Eli Lilly / Ventyx sealed prediction (base ECS 38 → delta to 57) is the canonical example.
clientJourney.costTime: Premium engagement tier · Senior partner availability required

clientJourney.description: Month 6: First verification checkpoint. Were predicted frictions materialised at FP1 (Days 30-60)? Are FP2 frictions emerging? Which leadership departures occurred? ECS score re-evaluated against actual evidence. Month 12: Second checkpoint — FP2 verification + early FP3 signal observation. Month 18: Third checkpoint — FP3 verification (departure trigger window). For sealed predictions: each checkpoint logged in ST_Prediction_Ledger_v1.xlsx. Result: case study created per corpus convention (8-section retrospective format) — calibration data flows back to ST_ECS_v2_canonical.xlsx and methodology v3.2.
clientJourney.projectFilesUsed: ST_B_Single_Output_Template_v1.xlsx (verification section in Section 19) + ST_Master_Resource_Database_v2.xlsx (calibration updates) + ST_Investment_Memorandum.docx OR ST_Investment_Memorandum_v3.docx (canonical version pending Phase 4 authorisation) + ST_Prediction_Ledger_v1.xlsx (sealed-prediction verification logs)
clientJourney.costTime: Month 6 / Month 12 / Month 18 · Retainer: ranges per engagement

clientJourney.phase: DECISION FORK after Step 3 (Layer 1 reconciliation): → ① CONVERGENT or ② PARTIAL CONVERGENCE with high confidence: client may accept directional pattern OR proceed to engagement (Step 3.5 → Step 4). → ③ ROLE-LEVEL SPLIT: client should proceed to engagement (analyst review essential). → ④-A or ④-B IRRESOLVABLE: practitioner escalation REQUIRED before any further engagement decision (per ST_Triage_Framework.xlsx Sheet 7_Practitioner_Escalation). → Triage Trigger 5 (reliability saturation) or Trigger 6 (contradiction blocking): skip Step 4 (35-Q will not resolve); route directly to Step 4.7 analyst review.

clientJourney.threeLayerPosition: Steps 4-8: $90K-$200K per engagement (canonical pricing per project documented standard) · delivered within 2 weeks of Step 4.7 (Layer 3 analyst review) completion · Sealed predictions premium tier (Step 7.5).

clientJourney.phase: Academy of Structural Typology · structural-typology.com · Client Journey v5.1 (v3.1 architecture) · May 2026

cells.1: v3.1 BUILD NOTES · Source v5.0 → v5.1 (v3.1 architecture)
